timing_method: MTLCommandBuffer.gpuEndTime - gpuStartTime (per-launch GPU timestamps, waitUntilCompleted each iter)
honest_scope: Apple-side MPS FP32 SGEMM baseline only — Nvidia-side comparator from RFC 067 pD is FP16 HGEMM (cuBLAS GemmEx + WMMA).

Cross-platform ratios below are FP32-MPS vs FP16-cuBLAS — informational, NOT apples-to-apples for absolute throughput. Apple FP16 MPS would roughly 1.5-2× FP32 throughput; that comparison deferred (FP16 dtype + accuracy nuances out of scope this cycle).
No hexa-emit GEMM Metal kernel exists yet → no hexa-side Metal GEMM column. That's the multi-session follow-up (mirrors RFC 071 P3+ for NVPTX side).
Variance is wider than ubu-2 cuBLAS run because Mac is a developer laptop with other processes; we report median + std + min/max.

apple_m3_peak_reference.notes: Apple M3 10-core GPU spec ~3.5 FP32 TFLOPS theoretical. Measured saturation ~1.70 TFLOPS = ~48% of peak at d=1024, climbing monotonically with shape (memory-bound at small d). Larger d (2048+) would likely push closer to peak; deferred for unified-memory size budget.

interpretation: MPS FP32 GEMM throughput climbs monotonically with shape: 1.03 → 1.35 → 1.56 → 1.67 → 1.70 TFLOPS as d goes 256→1024. Classic compute-bound asymptote; reaches ~48% of M3's ~3.5 FP32 TFLOPS advertised peak.

Nvidia RTX 5070 cuBLAS FP16 HGEMM is 4.4× (d=256) to 32× (d=1024) the MPS FP32 latency — but this is a dtype-mismatched comparison. FP16 doubles arithmetic density on tensor cores, so the apples-to-apples penalty is roughly half: M3 FP32 ≈ 1/15 RTX 5070 FP32 at d=1024.

Establishes the Apple-side vendor-library baseline for future flame-on-Metal work. When a hexa-emit Metal SGEMM kernel exists, its TFLOPS will be compared to this MPS column (analogous to hexa wmma vs cuBLAS column in RFC 067 pD).

Latency std is high at d≥512 (0.10-0.42 ms) because the Mac is a shared-developer laptop with background processes; median is more reliable than mean. To tighten variance, run on a quiesced machine or rerun with timed=200.
followup: hexa-emit Metal SGEMM kernel (mirrors hexa wmma for NVPTX) — multi-session.
FP16 MPS pass with deterministic dtype-aware oracle (defer; expect ~1.5-2× the FP32 throughput on M3).
d=2048+ shapes (unified-memory budget: 12 MB per matrix at d=2048 FP32 — feasible on 16 GB M3; defer to dedicated shape-sweep cycle).
Quiesced-laptop rerun with timed=200 to tighten the d≥512 variance.
